---
title: "HALT (ERS 8): Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired"
format: html
---
# HALT (ERS 8): Hungry, Angry, Lonely, Tired

HALT is a quick risk check used when trying to stay alcohol-free. Hunger, anger, loneliness, and fatigue each lower coping capacity and increase impulsive risk. When two or more HALT states are active, risk often rises sharply.

HALT is most useful when used preventively, not only during crisis.

HALT works as a preventive scan for high-risk states. Catching these states early allows quick corrective action before urges escalate or clear thinking gets harder.

## HALT Check-In
| State | Current level (0-10) | Immediate corrective action |
|---|---:|---|
| Hungry |  |  |
| Angry |  |  |
| Lonely |  |  |
| Tired |  |  |

::: {.callout-important}
### Practice plan

Work through this in real-life situations rather than only reading it.

Do a HALT check twice daily for one week.

If something feels hard, simplify the step and repeat it consistently.
:::
